summary:
Claim: A p-value less than 0.10 indicates the result is unlikely to have occurred by random chance alone.  
Evidence: - P-values measure the probability of observing data as extreme as the result if the null hypothesis is true. - A p-value < 0.10 means such an outcome would happen by chance ≤10% of the time under the null hypothesis.  
Answer: D
